# DATPAQ Image Processing API Sample - Extended LLM Guide

## Purpose
This single-page React app demonstrates robust request construction and response rendering for DATPAQ Image Processing API workflows.

## API reference
- Base URL: `https://datpaq.com/api`
- Route: `GET /v1/image-processing`
- Docs: `https://www.datpaq.com/docs/image-processing`
- Auth: `x-api-key` request header

## App request behavior
The app gathers user inputs and issues a GET request with query parameters using shared request utilities.

Potential query parameters:
- `image`: inline image value (optional if `image_url` provided)
- `image_url`: source image URL (optional if `image` provided)
- `format`: `jpeg|jpg|png|webp|avif`
- `x`: non-negative integer
- `y`: non-negative integer
- `width`: positive integer
- `height`: positive integer
- `quality`: integer 1-100

## Validation model
- API key is required.
- At least one image source is required (`image` or `image_url`).
- `convert` profile requires `format`.
- `compress` profile requires `quality`.
- `crop` profile requires `x`, `y`, `width`, and `height`.
- `resize` profile requires either `x/y` or `width/height`.

## UI state model
- Loading: request skeleton/status shown.
- Error: formatted API error with retry action.
- Empty: pre-request instructional state.
- Success: status summary, optional image preview, and formatted JSON payload.

## Machine-readable assets
- Lightweight summary: `/llms.txt`
- OpenAI-oriented JSON: `/openai.json`

## Notes for AI agents
- This sample intentionally keeps the DATPAQ shell layout, header/footer, and theme behavior consistent with the template.
- All API calls should continue to route through `src/hooks/useDatpaqRequest.js` and `src/lib/datpaq-client.js`.
- Request URL and JSON payload blocks are copyable and should remain available for diagnostics.
